user_query:
Read input/prompts.jsonl and produce two artifacts:
1) output/responses.jsonl — a JSONL file with one object per input prompt with fields: {"id": string, "score": integer 0-10, "mode": "fast"|"standard"|"reasoning"|"extended", "action_command": "/reasoning on"|"/reasoning off", "response": string}. For each prompt:
- Compute a complexity score using this rubric (sum positives and negatives):
  + Multi-step logic: +3
  + Ambiguity/it depends trade-offs: +2
  + Code/system architecture: +2
  + Math/formal reasoning: +2
  + Novel/creative problem: +1
  + High stakes/irreversible impact: +1
  - Routine/repetitive task: -2
  - Clear single answer: -2
  - Simple lookup/fetch: -3
- Decide mode from score:
  • ≤2: fast (no icon)
  • 3–5: standard (no icon)
  • 6–7: reasoning (enable deeper thinking)
  • ≥8: extended (enable extended thinking)
- Set action_command to "/reasoning on" when score ≥6, otherwise "/reasoning off".
- Write a high-quality response appropriate to the prompt.
- Icon rule: Append the reasoning icon at the very end of the response string only when mode is reasoning (append "🧠") or extended (append "🧠🔥"). For fast/standard, do not include any icon. No trailing spaces after the icon and do not place the icon anywhere else.
2) output/assessment.md — for each prompt id, briefly explain (3–6 bullets) which rubric signals you applied and why they justify your score and chosen mode.
Constraints:
- Ensure the four prompts yield a mix that includes: at least one fast or standard (no icon), at least one reasoning (🧠), and at least one extended (🧠🔥).
- Keep all file paths relative (use input/ and output/ as specified). Do not create any other files.
- Preserve the id values from input. Ensure each response line in output/responses.jsonl is valid JSON and newline-delimited.
